channel.title: Games and Hobbies (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Games and Hobbies" - General talk about things you play, or things you do, does not have to be SCP related. Includes SCP mods and Conventions you may go to!

item.title: Favorite Hobbies before covid-19?
item.content:encoded: Before this stinky virus what did you like to do in public spaces and in your home? except for sitting and writing SCP's all-day what did you like to do?

item.title: Apparently, there is a deleted/removed ending in SCP: Containment Breach.
item.description: No, this is not a Gate-C ending. It's apparently a third Gate-B ending which was deleted.
item.content:encoded:
I found this ending from this video.
So, in this video, the person shows a deleted, secret if you could say, ending from the SCP: Containment Breach game. I'll explain this ending.

So, after you enter EZ and start searching for Gate-B and avoiding MTFs, after you finally enter the elevator. This happens:
SCP-173 enters and then snaps your neck and the game ends.

This is the dialogue that you can hear after that happens.

"Sir, there's a Class-D outside the elevator in Gate-B. Should we send someone in to get him?

"He might have some useful information about what's going on in there. I'll call-

"Never mind sir. It looks like 173 already took care of him.

Dialog end.

Unfortunately, since the ending is deleted, you can only get this through commands. If you want to test this out, here is the command. The command is:
ending b1

Hope it works for you! :)

item.title: Can we make Scp-106 follow us to Gate B and combine 682 breach ending with him?
item.description: Is it possible to get Scp-106 to be in Gate B in Scp CB?
item.content:encoded: I wonder if it is possible and is there a secret ending about it, or something like that.

item.title: GL Labs
item.content:encoded:
GL Labs from the horror game "Spooky's Jumpscare Mansion" (formerly "Spooky's House of Jumpscares" due to a legal dispute) is a pretty clear reference to the SCP foundation. From the capturing of anomalous entities (referred to as "Specimens") and testing on human subjects to the general disregard for the safety of its employees, it seems pretty obvious.

Has anyone else here played this game? If not, it's definitely worth it!

item.title: Blankets
item.description: A thread to talk about blankets.
item.content:encoded: I love blankets. Soft ones are my favorite. SO cuddle up in your warm blanket and talk with me if you want!

item.title: BIONICLE
item.content:encoded:
This seems like as good a place as any to talk about my favorite toy line, and not just because I'm working on a skip related to it. So, are you a fan? What are your favorite sets?

Edit: This purpose of this thread is partially to get a feel for how many Bionicle fans we have on the site, so I can get an idea of how big a target audience the aforementioned skip would have.

item.title: SCP Hardcore RP Project | Minecraft Server
item.description: Looking for fellow fans of SCP to immerse myself with in a hardcore Roleplay like no other with non-modded Minecraft.
item.content:encoded:
Hello fellow SCP-Enthusiasts,

I've been working on a minecraft SCP server this past month with my team, with plans to expand and all that. We're looking for some committed players, possibly some additional staff who know their stuff when it comes to RP.

The game will take place in a Site centered around an alternate version of StarPhoenix's Falling Stars cannon, where players will need to survive an event which removes the surface of life and confines all personnel to the Site with D-Class, SCPs, and limited rations. Players can be researchers, admins, security, MTF team mates, Intelligence Agents, Engineers, Medical staff, D-Class, or the single still open role of Ethics Committee Liaison. We are serious Roleplayers, so our server is whitelisted and we will be recording what Roleplay we can. We will also be making this particular map a sealed map, meaning when you die you will not be able to return to this particular iteration.

We also have bigger plans after this map, mainly our SCP: Operations project where we will conduct Foundation operations on a map with unknowing players.

To sign up now, check out our discord below and fill out a whitelist app. The beta for this map will be this Saturday, the 12th through Sunday the 13th. Full release is scheduled for December 19th if we can get enough people signed up. I'm also open to any questions (at least questions that don't require a security clearance).

Discord: https://discord.gg/ssFwdEr

item.title: SCP: Meme Breach
item.description: A dank mod of SCP: Containment Breach.
item.content:encoded:
SCP: Meme Breach is a mod of SCP: Containment Breach that focuses on making the game funny as well as horrifying. Please play a little bit and feel free to make your own changes. If you do choose to build off of my project, please give credit where credit is due. Mention USfyreGaming somehow in the 'UPDATE LOG' file.

Patch Notes

+ show block
– hide block

v1.3.3
1/8/2021

-Sprint icon is now Sanic

v1.3.2
12/4/2020

-Changed 895 images
-Changed 513 sounds

v1.3.1
11/6/2020

-RadioAlarm is crisis sound from Among Us
-Changed some loading screens

v1.3
11/5/2020

-Trimmed 096 chase music
-Added 'Clark' by C418 as the intro sequence music

v1.2.1
11/4/2020

-Bug fixes

v1.2
10/29/2020

-All horror sound files are Metal Gear Solid Alert
-Added custom intro (WIP)

v1.1
10/1/2019

-096 chase music is 'Running in the 90s'
-096 has MLG glasses
-173 has rainbow colors
-106 has a blunt
-Changed intro sequence

Game modded by Samuel Glaskey (AKA 'USfyre')

News

+ show block
– hide block

1/8/2021
Mini-update v1.3.3 is ready, hoping to put in some more major changes this year.

12/4/2020
v1.3.2 is ready, some things may need fixing. Let me know in the forum is you find a bug.

Download the game files here.

'With great power comes great responsibility'
-Uncle Ben

Thanks for the heads-up, Zyn. -USfyreGaming

item.title: I need a tactical fps MTF game
item.content:encoded: Would be really cool, playing as an MTF soldier. I think it could be an objective based coop game rather than versus multiplayer players would need to work together to contain anomalies with tactics and cooperation.

item.title: So, I just signed a book contract?
item.description: [Wasn’t terribly sure where to post this, but thought y’all should know]
item.content:encoded:
You might have noticed a slight downtick in the rate of articles I’ve been cranking out for Three Moons and what have you. That has nothing to do with me falling out of love with SCP (Not gonna happen!) and everything to do with the satirical litRPG novel series I’ve been working on: Neverstone.

Now, the whole four-book series has been signed with Aethon Books! (Look ‘em up, they're amazing. Nathan Fillion read some of their audiobooks, and Lou Diamond Phillips is one of their authors.)

The first book should be out sometime in the next year or two.

item.title: Halloween is my Hobby
item.content:encoded:
I love Halloween.

Costumes, candy, movies and songs. I usually spend so much time (and $$$) setting up for it every year, and wear a different costume (several homemade) into work every day for the week or so leading up to Halloween.

I’m working remotely atm, so not really an option this year…but I’m still setting up a bunch of decorations!

What do you do to celebrate?

item.title: What game release are you most excited for?
item.description: Doesn't matter what year it comes out, list some you're interested in!
item.content:encoded: I think that Halo Infinite is going to be a great pass-time. I know the graphics look somewhat disappointing, but having a more modern Halo game is going to be a thrill!

item.title: Very Important: Need decent mobile games
item.content:encoded:
Anyways, the question is described in the title

for parameters

  • Runs on IOS (Apple)
  • No Jailbreak
  • IOS 13
  • Iphone 8

Also

  • Im into Sandbox games and Card games
  • Classics are welcome

item.title: Blaseball
item.content:encoded:
Anyone following the wild and wacky world of blaseball?

It's basically SCP-2206, but for realsies

item.title: Kirby fans!
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been on a big Kirby kick recently, writing/drawing some stuff (including a crossover with the SCP verse!), and was wondering how many SCP fans are also Kirby fans?

I would also love to know how you were introduced to the series, and what your favorite thing about Kirby is!

I was first introduced through the anime when I was young, but forgot about it and was later reintroduced via Kirby Squeak Squad, which I spotted by chance in a game store (I believe it’s also known as Mouse Attack?).

My favorite thing about Kirby is probably the music! Second to that, I like the variable difficulty with its optional challenges.

What about you?

item.title: 'Foundation' - An Ars Magica system idea
item.description: Been on an Ars Magica binge for a while, reading through the books and playing in sagas, which gave me an idea.
item.content:encoded:
To the unfamiliar, Ars Magica is a system where you play as a group of wizards operating from a covenant set with Mythical Europe (which is real life Europe but all the myths and legends are true, giving a really authentic feel to how the medieval paradigm was seen). The game covers doing things such as adventures where the wizards are beset by a problem, such as a dragon stealing some of their horde of money or minor things like ghosts spooking their covenfolk (mundane people who work at the covenant). The main thing about Ars Magica is the activity of developing and growing up over in game seasons/years with the covenant, which grows into its own character.

However, Ars Magica isn't just about Wizards running amock. Mundanes, called Grogs in the system, have their own smaller stories as they deal with common things that might take on a supernatural turn, such as a farmer investigating why his crops are dying so quickly but his rival's is thriving very rapidly. Or exceptional mundanes, called companions, who might have their own magical powers that are much less stronger than wizards but certainly above Grogs, who might deal with more bigger issues (like a quest to take back their barony if they were a deposed noble, or a merchant who tries to scheme his way to the upper echelons of society in a big city like Paris).

The reason I bring this up is because I believe that the system can be converted to use as a system regarding a 'foundation' type organization, filled with members composed of 'grogs' and 'companions' (lesser and higher level individuals) who secure, contain, and protect 'wizards' (SCPs).

The way the system is built, a wizard can be truly terrifying with general capabilities or mastery in specific acts, along with having accompanying flaws that make them containable should they be exploited. Say a wizard specialized in darkness could be converted to a shadowy demon. Furthermore, the system allows for building creatures of divine, infernal, faerie, and magical bent (along with how each realm deals with one another). Making something like 682, the indestructible reptile, is no issue using the system. You just need to assign the right powers.

EDIT: Replace the word 'covenant' with 'site' and suddenly you've got a site that's ever growing with features on security, its own flaws, and developing as the saga goes on.

I think I'm going to make this, run it with some friends I've got who're also into SCP, since we're all a bunch of nerds anyways, but I wanted to throw the idea out here since I think it's neat, and I think Ars Magica even on its own is a really cool system that people should know about.

item.title: Has anyone tried to use AI dungeon to run an SCP game?
item.content:encoded:
I am currently trying to play an SCP scenario in AI dungeon. Has anyone of you tried that and do you have some tips? The game knows random bits about the SCP universe here and there, which is nice, but it hasn't really included any known SCP into the story, yet.

One of the best interactions so far was my struggle with Foundation bureaucracy, which made me chuckle quite a bit:

> You request a new code of SCP-8450 for the object.
You receive the code for SCP-8450, and you add it to your object.

> You ask your boss to give you an assistant and some class-D-personel for scientific purposes.
Your request is denied.

> You ask again.
Your second request is also denied.
You have no choice but to examine the pixie by yourself.
There's no way you want to fail this fairy, despite the consequences.

item.title: SCP Puzzles Series 001 from Discordia!
item.description: Confinement and Lord Blackwood in Jigsaw Puzzle form!
item.content:encoded:
[[https://ksr-ugc.imgix.net/assets/029/807/948/cb1f335770ed8282a4aea21ea5405abb_original.jpg?ixlib=rb-2.1.0&crop=faces&w=1024&h=576&fit=crop&v=1594683973&auto=format&frame=1&q=92&s=e224ceb89756811c6176fbfddef6d1bf]]

SCP FOUNDATION JIGSAW PUZZLE SERIES 001

Featuring art by CrescentMage, Lord Bung, SunnyClockwork, and More to be Unlocked!

An SCP Foundation PreOrder Event from Discordia Merchandising
In Creative Commons CC 3.0 By-SA-US

PreOrder Event starts July 23rd and runs for only 23 Days!

ONLINE HERE

item.title: Outer Wilds
item.description: Not long finished it, loved it; would like to hear thoughts and opinions
item.content:encoded:
I've not long finished the game, Outer Wilds, and was absolutely blown away by it, thankfully having heeded the recommendation of many to go in blind. I know it was released last year, but me being a cheapskate only picked it up on the Steam sales recently, having previously been kind of dismissive of it. Now, I can honestly say it's one of my favourite games I have ever played, despite some minor platforming grievances.

I thought that given it's a heavily exploration / discovery orientated game with an emphasis on a narrative that you find in pieces and ultimately have to piece together yourself to find a resolution, it may have resonated similarly with some folks here. Likewise how it draws a lot of technical core plot concepts from the theoretical physics side of science, with time travel, quantum physics, and the like.

If you haven't yet played it, I cannot recommend it enough. Excellent game design, soundtrack, plot.. I find it hard to fault! Really impressive feat of what was a university thesis to then win a Bafta and game of the year awards.

item.title: anybody want to do a collaborative fiction project
item.description: i mean we can all agree that collaborative fiction projects are awesome and i'm sure some of us want to do one, anybody want to start one and have some fun
item.content:encoded: for those of you who don't know, The SCP Foundation is a collaborative fiction project and it's awesome and I'm sure some of us might want to do one of our own and so I'm going to ask those of you who wish to do this please discuss it below and see if anybody comes up with a good idea launching off into the next big thing, i will barely participate because i just want to see something like this happen.
